danger_signals.visual_form: mutated animals and ghouls rendered in isometric sprite form with aggressive animation states
danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: wasteland areas with scorched/barren environmental textures and altered color palette (yellows, browns)
danger_signals.meaning: environmental hazard causing damage over time

danger_signals.visual_form: distinct armor/clothing visual variants (raiders, super mutants) with hostile stance animations
PIP-Boy 2000 display showing quest timers (150 in-game days for main quest)
danger_signals.meaning: urgent objective requiring completion before world-state failure

safety_signals.visual_form: underground bunker aesthetic with clean blue/grey technology and orderly layout
safety_signals.meaning: starting safe zone, sanctuary from wasteland threats

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs with friendly stance animations and dialogue options without hostile cues
safety_signals.meaning: non-hostile actor available for trade, quests, or companionship

safety_signals.visual_form: clustered buildings and structures with NPCs conducting non-combat animations
safety_signals.meaning: community zones with reduced immediate danger

safety_signals.visual_form: 'talking head' 3D character portraits appearing during interaction, multiple dialogue options displayed
safety_signals.meaning: peaceful interaction mode active, diplomacy possible

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: underground vault with characteristic 1950s retro-futuristic decor

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: distinctive humanoid mutant silhouette, oversized build
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: hostile stance, heavy armor or combat posture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: vault installations corrupted by FEV (Forced Evolutionary Virus), mutant strongholds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: cobbled armor aesthetic, post-apocalyptic makeshift gear
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: aggressive animation states, varied crude weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: camps, settlements taken by force, barricaded structures

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: feral ghouls vs sentient ghouls (dialogue available for latter)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: irradiated zones, vaults contaminated by radiation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vault 13 environment shows signs of resource strain; NPCs may reference water shortage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vault Overseer maintains urgent quest directive; 150-day time limit enforced via PIP-Boy UI

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: unexplored map areas show fog-of-war or blank space on PIP-Boy 2000 display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs unable to provide information about unknown regions; encounter tables favor weaker enemies in starting zone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: particular faction armor/symbols prevalent in specific regions; environmental decay matches faction type (super mutant scorched areas, raider camps)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC encounters skewed toward dominant faction; settlement NPCs show fear or hostility toward player based on local faction control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: color shift in affected areas toward sickly yellows/greens; mutated flora and fauna rendering; environmental detail shows decay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: creatures in contaminated zones display mutations; NPCs avoid highly radiated areas; damage tick occurs without protection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: PIP-Boy quest log updates; environment may show changes (cleared raider camp becomes safe, settlement rebuilds)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC dialogue shifts based on quest state; reputation system adjusts NPC hostility/friendliness; alternative paths unlock via completion state

environmental_storytelling.example: orderly vault interior suggests hierarchical, managed society; chaotic raider camp or ghoul-infested area suggests lawlessness and decay
well-armed super mutant stronghold vs. poorly-equipped settler camp communicates power dynamics and resource distribution
NPCs in successful settlements show idle animations of work/socializing; those in threatened settlements show defensive postures or stress behaviors
wasteland areas show increasing structural collapse and radiation scarring as player ventures further from vault; distant regions visually hostile
significant NPCs rendered with 3D talking heads showing age, scarring, faction-aligned outfit details, communicating character role and background
Vault-Tec structures maintain 1950s aesthetic; super mutant bases show crude fortification; raider camps use scavenged materials asymmetrically

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: can engage in combat; NPCs display caution or aggression based on reputation and threat assessment
sprite animation shifts to aggressive stance; moves toward player; attack indicators display in turn-based combat UI
sprite mirrors player movement at offset position; participates in combat against enemies; leaves formation if player enters dialogue or stealth
performs idle animation loops (standing, occasionally moving short distances); displays friendly dialogue options; no combat stance
may initiate dialogue challenge; hostile stance if player reputation with faction is low; friendly approach if reputation is high
displays dialogue options despite mutated appearance; sprite animation neutral; can become ally or informant
attacks on sight in stronghold; sprite animation aggressive; will pursue player across multiple tiles; no dialogue option with feral variants

notes: Fallout (1997) employs isometric trimetric perspective for world visualization, making faction/danger identification heavily reliant on sprite differentiation, armor/equipment visual design, and environmental color/decay states. The retro-futuristic 1950s Atomic Age aesthetic is fundamental to world-state communication—clean vault interiors contrast sharply with wasteland decay. The PIP-Boy 2000 UI serves dual function as both diegetic character interface and non-diegetic quest/map display, making world-state information accessible without breaking immersion. Reputation system is communicated through NPC dialogue tone and behavioral stance rather than explicit faction bars. The 150-day time limit creates persistent tension signaled through quest log UI rather than environmental visual degradation of vault condition.
